content.problemStatement:
Cricket is almost like a religion in India and many young kids attend coaching camps every year. This year there are total N kids at the camp and the coach is forming teams so that kids within a team can practice and compete with each other. As we know that kids are afraid of tall and heavy persons, they are afraid to get in to a team having such player. More formally, kid A is afraid of another kid B if weight(A) ≤ weight(B) and height(A) ≤ height(B). You can assume that no two kids have both height and weight equal to each other. Here is how the teams are formed by the coach.



All the N students are lined up. For R = 1,2,3,... in order, in the Rth round the coach calls all the students who are not afraid of any one in the line. They all come forward, form a team number R and leave for practice. This process is repeated as long as there are kids still in the line, yet to form a team. Given the height and weight of each of the N students, find the number of teams formed and the number of kids in each of them.
constraints: No two kids have both height and weight equal to each other
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing N, the number of students. Each of the following N lines contains two integers Hi and Wi, the height and weight of the ith student.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the total number of teams formed, followed by the number of kids in each team, separated by spaces.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the students are selected in two teams. The first team has one student with height 5 and weight 5. The second team has two students with heights 3 and 4, and weights 4 and 3 respectively. For the second test case, the students are selected in four teams. The first team has one student with height 7 and weight 7. The second team has two students with heights 5 and 3, and weights 5 and 4 respectively. The third team has one student with height 1 and weight 2. The fourth team has two students with heights 3 and 2, and weights 4 and 1 respectively.
Each student is selected in a separate team.
All students are selected in the same team.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by sorting the students based on their heights and weights. Then, we iterate through the sorted list and select the students who are not afraid of any other student. We repeat this process until all students have been selected.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) per test case, O(T * N log N) total. The sorting operation takes O(N log N) time, and we perform this operation for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the array of students.

code.Python3:
import sys

class Student:
    def __init__(self, height, weight):
        self.height = height
        self.weight = weight

    def __lt__(self, other):
        if self.height != other.height:
            return self.height < other.height
        return self.weight < other.weight

T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    students = [Student(*map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(N)]
    students.sort()
    teams = 0
    teamSizes = [0] * N
    teamIndex = 0
    while teamIndex < N:
        teamSize = 0
        maxWeight = 0
        maxHeight = 0
        for i in range(teamIndex, N):
            if students[i].weight > maxWeight or students[i].height > maxHeight:
                teamSize += 1
                maxWeight = students[i].weight
                maxHeight = students[i].height
        teamSizes[teams] = teamSize
        teams += 1
        teamIndex += teamSize
    print(teams)
    print(' '.join(map(str, teamSizes[:teams])))
